---
title: "475p"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(survminer)
library(survival)
library("foreign")

data <- read.csv("bone-marrow.csv")


data[data == "-1"] <- NA
data[data == "1e+06"] <- NA


categorical_cols <- c("Recipientgender", "Stemcellsource", "Donorage35", "IIIV", 
                     "Gendermatch", "DonorABO", "RecipientABO", "RecipientRh", 
                     "ABOmatch", "CMVstatus", "DonorCMV", "RecipientCMV", 
                     "Disease", "Riskgroup", "Txpostrelapse", "Diseasegroup",
                     "HLAmatch", "HLAmismatch", "Antigen", "Alel", "HLAgrI",
                     "Recipientage10", "Recipientageint", "Relapse", 
                     "aGvHDIIIIV", "extcGvHD", "survival_status")


for(col in categorical_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(data)) {
    data[[col]] <- as.factor(data[[col]])
  }
}



numeric_cols <- names(data)[sapply(data, is.numeric)]
numeric_cols <- setdiff(numeric_cols, categorical_cols)  


for(col in numeric_cols) {
  
  Q1 <- quantile(data[[col]], 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  Q3 <- quantile(data[[col]], 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  
  
  lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  
  
  data[[col]][data[[col]] < lower_bound | data[[col]] > upper_bound] <- NA
}


na_ratio <- rowSums(is.na(data)) / ncol(data)

data_cleaned <- data[na_ratio <= 0.3, ]


for(col in numeric_cols) {
  median_val <- median(data_cleaned[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  data_cleaned[[col]][is.na(data_cleaned[[col]])] <- median_val
}


for(col in categorical_cols) {
  if(col %in% names(data_cleaned)) {
    mode_val <- names(sort(table(data_cleaned[[col]]), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
    data_cleaned[[col]][is.na(data_cleaned[[col]])] <- mode_val
  }
}


data_cleaned$survival_time[data_cleaned$survival_time < 0] <- NA
data_cleaned$survival_time <- as.numeric(data_cleaned$survival_time)


write.csv(data_cleaned, "final_cleaned_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)


cleaning_report <- list(
  original_rows = nrow(data),
  cleaned_rows = nrow(data_cleaned),
  removed_rows = nrow(data) - nrow(data_cleaned),
  na_before = sum(is.na(data)),
  na_after = sum(is.na(data_cleaned))
)

print("Data Cleaning Report:")
print(paste("Original number of rows:", cleaning_report$original_rows))
print(paste("Final number of rows:", cleaning_report$cleaned_rows))
print(paste("Removed rows:", cleaning_report$removed_rows))
print(paste("NAs before cleaning:", cleaning_report$na_before))
print(paste("NAs after cleaning:", cleaning_report$na_after))


summary(data_cleaned)
```

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(survival)
library(glmnet)
library(survminer)
library(dplyr)

# Read data
data <- read.csv("final_cleaned_data.csv")

# Convert Disease to factor with explicit levels
data$Disease <- factor(data$Disease)
print("Disease categories:")
print(levels(data$Disease))

# Convert other character variables to factors
data <- data %>% mutate_if(is.character, as.factor)

# Ensure survival_status is numeric
data$survival_status <- as.numeric(as.character(data$survival_status))

# 1. Univariate Cox analysis
univariate_cox <- function(data, time_col, status_col, feature) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(", time_col, ",", status_col, ") ~", feature))
  fit <- tryCatch({
    coxph(formula, data = data)
  }, error = function(e) {
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if(!is.null(fit)) {
    summary <- summary(fit)
    # Handle multi-level factors
    if(length(coef(fit)) > 1) {
      results <- data.frame(
        feature = paste(feature, names(coef(fit)), sep="_"),
        hazard_ratio = exp(coef(fit)),
        p_value = summary$coefficients[,5],
        concordance = summary$concordance[1]
      )
    } else {
      results <- data.frame(
        feature = feature,
        hazard_ratio = exp(coef(fit)),
        p_value = summary$coefficients[,5],
        concordance = summary$concordance[1]
      )
    }
    return(results)
  }
  return(NULL)
}

# Get predictors
predictors <- names(data)[!names(data) %in% c("survival_time", "survival_status")]

# Perform univariate Cox analysis
cox_results <- list()
for(predictor in predictors) {
  result <- univariate_cox(data, "survival_time", "survival_status", predictor)
  if(!is.null(result)) {
    cox_results[[predictor]] <- result
  }
}

# Combine results and sort by p-value
cox_results_df <- do.call(rbind, cox_results)
cox_results_df <- cox_results_df[order(cox_results_df$p_value), ]

# 2. LASSO Cox regression
# Create model matrix with proper handling of factors
X <- model.matrix(~ . - survival_time - survival_status, data = data)[,-1]
y <- Surv(data$survival_time, data$survival_status)

# Perform LASSO
set.seed(123)
cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(X, y, family = "cox", alpha = 1)
lasso_fit <- glmnet(X, y, family = "cox", lambda = cv_fit$lambda.min)

# Get LASSO-selected features
lasso_coef <- coef(lasso_fit)
selected_features_lasso <- rownames(lasso_coef)[which(lasso_coef != 0)]

# 3. Combine results
# Get significant features from univariate analysis
significant_univariate <- cox_results_df$feature[cox_results_df$p_value < 0.05]

# Create final feature set
final_features <- unique(c(significant_univariate, selected_features_lasso))

# Print results
cat("\nSignificant features from univariate Cox regression (p < 0.05):\n")
print(cox_results_df[cox_results_df$p_value < 0.05, ])

cat("\nFeatures selected by LASSO:\n")
print(selected_features_lasso)

# 4. Final Cox model
# Create formula ensuring proper handling of categorical variables
predictors_formula <- paste(predictors, collapse=" + ")
final_formula <- as.formula(paste("Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~", predictors_formula))

# Fit final model
final_model <- coxph(final_formula, data = data)
print(summary(final_model))

# Save results
selected_data <- data[, c(predictors, "survival_time", "survival_status")]
write.csv(selected_data, "cox_selected_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)


```

```{r}
print(final_features)

# Create survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = selected_data$survival_time, event = selected_data$survival_status)

cox_model <- coxph(surv_obj ~ Disease + Rbodymass + Relapse + Recipientage + CD3dkgx10d8 + CD34kgx10d6 + aGvHDIIIIV + Recipientage10 + Donorage35 + RecipientRh , data = selected_data)

summary(cox_model)
```

```{r}
cox_model <- coxph(surv_obj ~ Disease + Relapse + aGvHDIIIIV + RecipientRh , data = selected_data)

summary(cox_model)
```

```{r}
# Check the proportional hazards assumption
ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_model)
print(ph_test)

# Plot Schoenfeld residuals for diagnostics
plot(ph_test)


# Generate survival curves based on the Cox model
ggsurv <- ggsurvplot(survfit(cox_model), 
                     data = selected_data, 
                     conf.int = TRUE, 
                     risk.table = TRUE, 
                     xlab = "Time (days)", 
                     ylab = "Survival Probability",
                     title = "Survival Curve from Cox Model")
print(ggsurv)

ggcoxdiagnostics(cox_model, type = "deviance", ggtheme = theme_minimal())
```

```{r}
library(forestplot)
library(dplyr)
library(broom)

# Extract and format data from the Cox model
# Using tidy() to convert model results into a clean dataframe
cox_data <- broom::tidy(cox_model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create readable English labels
    label = case_when(
      term == "Disease" ~ "Disease Status",
      term == "Relapse" ~ "Relapse Status",
      term == "aGvHDIIIIV" ~ "Acute GVHD III-IV",
      term == "RecipientRh" ~ "Recipient Rh Factor",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    # Format HR values and confidence intervals
    estimate_text = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f-%.2f)", 
                          exp(estimate),  # HR value
                          exp(conf.low),  # 95% CI lower bound
                          exp(conf.high)  # 95% CI upper bound
    )
  )

# Create the forest plot data matrix
# First column: variable labels, Second column: HR values and CI
forestplot_matrix <- cbind(
  c("Risk Factors", cox_data$label),  # Column 1: Variable names
  c("HR (95% CI)", cox_data$estimate_text)  # Column 2: HR values and CI
)

# Create the forest plot
forestplot(
  # Basic data settings
  forestplot_matrix,  # Text matrix to display
  mean = c(NA, exp(cox_data$estimate)),  # HR point estimates
  lower = c(NA, exp(cox_data$conf.low)),  # CI lower bounds
  upper = c(NA, exp(cox_data$conf.high)),  # CI upper bounds
  
  # Graph layout settings
  boxsize = 0.3,  # Size of boxes
  graphwidth = unit(4, "inches"),  # Width of graph
  
  # Title settings
  title = "Forest Plot of Risk Factors for Survival Analysis",
  subtitle = paste("Based on Cox Proportional Hazards Model\n",
                  format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")),
  
  # Color settings
  col = fpColors(
    box = "#2E86C1",      # Box color: deep blue
    lines = "#2E86C1",    # Line color
    zero = "#CB4335",     # Reference line color: dark red
    summary = "#1B4F72"   # Summary statistics color: darker blue
  ),
  
  # Reference line settings
  zero = 1,  # Reference line at HR=1
  
  # Text format settings
  txt_gp = fpTxtGp(
    ticks = gpar(cex = 0.85),    # Tick text size
    xlab = gpar(cex = 0.9),      # x-axis label size
    title = gpar(cex = 1.2)      # Title size
  ),
  
  # Helper element settings
  grid = TRUE,  # Show grid lines
  
  # x-axis settings
  xticks = c(0.5, 1, 2, 4),  # x-axis tick values
  xlog = TRUE  # Use logarithmic scale
)

```

```{r}

ggforest(cox_model, data = data, main = "Hazard Ratio (HR) Forest Plot") 
```



```{r}
cox_resids <- resid(cox_model, type = "deviance")
# 3. Plot deviance residuals vs risk score
tibble(risk_score = predict(cox_model), deviance_resids = cox_resids) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = risk_score, y = deviance_resids)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  xlab("Risk Score") +
  ylab("Deviance Residuals")

# 4. Q-Q plot of deviance residuals
qqnorm(cox_resids, ylab = "Deviance Residuals", xlab = "N(0,1) Quantiles")
abline(0, 1, lty = 2)
```

```{r}
library(survival)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(survminer)
library(foreign)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)

patients <- read.arff("bone-marrow.arff")
# write.csv(patients, "bone-marrow.csv")



# Load and clean the data


patients <- read.csv("bone-marrow.csv")
patients <- patients %>%
  mutate(survival_time = survival_time/30,
    CD34kgx10d6 = as.factor(ifelse(CD34kgx10d6 >= mean(CD34kgx10d6),"high","low")),
    Gendermatch = as.factor(Gendermatch),
    Stemcellsource = as.factor(Stemcellsource),
    Diseasegroup = as.factor(case_when(Diseasegroup == 1 ~"malignant", Diseasegroup == 0 ~ "not malignant")),
    Riskgroup = as.factor(Riskgroup),
    HLAmismatch = as.factor(HLAmismatch),
    ABOmatch=as.factor(ABOmatch)
    # Antigen = as.factor(as.numeric(Antigen) + 1),
    ) |>na.omit()


#EDA (scatterplot, and others relevant plots)
eda <- patients |> mutate (survival_status = as.factor(survival_status)) |> select(survival_status,survival_time,Diseasegroup, Stemcellsource, Gendermatch, CD34kgx10d6)
# eda0 <- ggplot(patients, aes(x = Riskgroup, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Survival Time by Low and High Risk",
#     x = "Risk Type",
#     y = "Survival Time",
#     fill = "Survival Status"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()


# eda1<-ggplot(patients, aes(x = Donorage, y = survival_time, color = survival_status)) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Survival Time vs Donor Age",
#     x = "Donor Age",
#     y = "Survival Time",
#     color = "Survival Status"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()

eda2<-ggplot(eda, aes(x = Gendermatch, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Time by Gender Match",
    x = "Gender Match",
    y = "Survival Time",
    fill = "Survival Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

eda3 <- ggplot(eda, aes(x = Stemcellsource, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Time by Stem\n Cell Source",
    x = "Stem Cell Source",
    y = "Survival Time",
    fill = "Survival Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
eda4 <- ggplot(eda, aes(x = Diseasegroup, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Time by Disease group",
    x = "Disease Type",
    y = "Survival Time",
    fill = "Survival Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14), axis.title.x = element_text(size=14), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),plot.caption = element_text(size=12))

eda5 <- ggplot(eda, aes(x = CD34kgx10d6, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Survival Time by Receipient\n CD34+ Groups",
    x = "CD34+ Types",
    y = "Survival Time",
    fill = "Survival Status", caption = "Notes: CD34⁺ cells are stem cells in bone marrow that regenerate blood cells and are crucial for bone marrow transplants in blood cancer treatment.\n Here we measure CD34⁺ concentration per kg of body weight of patients and group CD34⁺ into group of high (above sample mean) concentration  \n and low (below or equal to sample mean) concentration."
  ) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14), axis.title.x = element_text(size=14), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),plot.caption = element_text(size=12))
(eda4|eda5)

# eda5 <- ggplot(patients, aes(x = HLAmismatch, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Survival Time by HLA mismatch",
#     x = "HLAmismatch",
#     y = "Survival Time",
#     fill = "Survival Status"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# eda6 <- ggplot(patients, aes(x = ABOmatch, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Survival Time by ABO match",
#     x = "ABOmatch",
#     y = "Survival Time",
#     fill = "Survival Status"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# eda7 <- ggplot(patients, aes(x = Antigen, y = survival_time, fill = survival_status)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Survival Time by Antigen Differences",
#     x = "Antigen Differences",
#     y = "Survival Time",
#     fill = "Survival Status", caption = "In how many antigens there is difference beetwen the donor nad the recipient\n (0 - no differences, 1 - one difference,2 - two (three(3)) differences)"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# (eda5|eda6)/eda7

#surfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~)



# residual analysis ()
```

```{r}
# KM.fit1 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ Donorage + Gendermatch + Stemcellsource, data = patients)
# 
# plot.KM.fit1 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit1, conf.int = FALSE,  risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Days", title = "K-M three predictors")


# KM.fit2 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ Riskgroup, data = patients)
# 
# plot.KM.fit2 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit2, conf.int = FALSE,  risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Days", title = "K-M Risk")

KM.fit3 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ Diseasegroup, data = patients)

plot.KM.fit3 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit3, conf.int = TRUE, risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Months", title = "K-M Disease Malignance", font.caption = 12)

plot.km3 <- plot.KM.fit3$plot +  geom_vline(xintercept = 12, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") + annotate("text", x = 12, y = 0.5, label = "Year 1", color = "red", angle = 90, vjust = -0.5) + annotate("text", x = 60, y = 0.5, label = "Year 5", color = "blue", angle = 90, vjust = -0.2)

KM.fit4 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ CD34kgx10d6, data = patients)

plot.KM.fit4 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit4, conf.int = TRUE, risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Months", title = "K-M CD34+ Group", caption = "Notes:This plots shows the distribution of Kaplan–Meier estimates for the patients with blood cancer in different strata (groups) disease malignance and groups\n of CD34+ cells in the range of about 120 months (10 years).", font.caption = 12)

plot.km4 <- plot.KM.fit4$plot +  geom_vline(xintercept = 12, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") + geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", color = "blue") + annotate("text", x = 12, y = 0.5, label = "Year 1", color = "red", angle = 90, vjust = -0.2) + annotate("text", x = 60, y = 0.5, label = "Year 5", color = "blue", angle = 90, vjust = -0.2)

(plot.km3|plot.km4)

# (plot.KM.fit2$plot|plot.KM.fit3$plot)
# 
# KM.fit4 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ HLAmismatch, data = patients)
# 
# plot.KM.fit4 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit4, conf.int = FALSE, risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Days", title = "K-M HLAmismatch")
# 
# KM.fit5 <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ ABOmatch, data = patients)
# 
# plot.KM.fit5 <- ggsurvplot(KM.fit5, conf.int = FALSE, risk.table = FALSE, xlab = "Days", title = "K-M ABO match")
# 
# (plot.KM.fit4$plot|plot.KM.fit5$plot)

```

```{r}

#disease group

KM.mag <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ 1, data = patients, subset = (Diseasegroup == "malignant"))
KM.nomag <- survfit(Surv(survival_time, survival_status)~ 1, data = patients, subset = (Diseasegroup == "not malignant"))

result2 <- tibble(
  time = rep(c(KM.mag$time, KM.nomag$time), 4),
  surv = rep(c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv), 4),
  grp = rep(c(rep("malignant", length(KM.mag$time)), rep("not malignant", length(KM.nomag$time))), 4),
  psi_inverse = c(
    -log(c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv)), # Exponential
    log(-log(c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv))), # Weibull
    qnorm(1 - c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv)), # Lognormal
    log((1 - c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv)) / c(KM.mag$surv, KM.nomag$surv)) # Log-logistic
  ),
  model = rep(c("exponential", "weibull", "lognormal", "loglogistic"), each = length(c(KM.mag$time, KM.nomag$time)))
) %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = c("exponential", "weibull", "lognormal", "loglogistic")))

# Modified code for your diagnostic plots
plot2 <- result2 |> 
  ggplot(aes(x=log(time), y=psi_inverse, col = grp, group = grp, pch = grp)) + 
  geom_point() +
  xlab(expression(log(t))) + 
  ylab(expression(paste(G^{-1}, "S(t)"))) +
  geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = F) +
  facet_grid(~model) + 
  scale_color_manual(name = "Group",
    values = c("malignant" = "#FF9999", "not malignant" = "#99CCFF"), 
    labels = c("Malignant", "Not Malignant")
  ) +
  labs(title ="Graphical Comparison of Model Fitness") +
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = "Group",
    values = c("malignant" = 16, "not malignant" = 17),
    labels = c("Malignant", "Not Malignant")) +
  theme_bw() + # Added theme_bw() for cleaner look
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

plot1 <- result1 |> 
  ggplot(aes(x=log(time), y=psi_inverse, col = grp, group = grp, pch = grp)) + 
  geom_point() +
  xlab(expression(log(t))) + 
  ylab(expression(paste(G^{-1}, "S(t)"))) +
  geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = F) +
  facet_grid(~model) + 
  scale_color_manual(name = "Group",
    values = c("high" = "#CCCCFF", "low" = "#99E6E6"), 
    labels = c("High\n concentration", "Low\n concentration")
  ) +
  labs(
    caption = "Notes: This plot shows the fitness of potential parametric models to the data on the\n malignance (upper figure) and CD34+ group (lower figure). The parallel lines\n with most points are aligned with the lines would indicate a comparative good\n fit, which is observed to be the lognormal model."
  ) +
  theme_bw() + # Added theme_bw()
  theme(
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) + 
  guides(shape = "none")

# Combine plots
(plot2/plot1)
```

```{r}
library(kableExtra)
# AFT
# exponential
# aft_model1 <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ Riskgroup, data = patients, dist = "lognormal")
# 
# summary(aft_model1)

#lognormal

aft_model2 <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ Diseasegroup, data = patients, dist = "lognormal")

summary(aft_model2)

aft_model2_mag <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ 1, data = patients, subset = (Diseasegroup=="malignant"), dist = "lognormal")
aft_model1_nomag <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ 1, data = patients, subset = (Diseasegroup=="not malignant"), dist = "lognormal")

aft_model3 <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ Diseasegroup + CD34kgx10d6, data = patients, dist = "lognormal")
summary(aft_model3)
# type by hands
model3_summary <- tibble(
  Term = c("(Intercept)", "not malignant", "low CD34+", "Log(scale)"),
  Value = c(8.774, 0.081, -1.186, 0.753),
  `Std. Error` = c(0.494, 0.638, 0.496, 0.119),
  z = c(17.76, 0.13, -2.39, 6.33),
  p = c("< 2e-16", 0.899, 0.017, "2.5e-10")
)
kable(model3_summary, caption = "Effects of Malignance and CD34+ Cells on Survival") %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 12) %>% footnote(general = "This table shows the summay of estimates and their standard errors from the lognormal parametric model for predicting the survival time of patients. The z (z-score) and p (p-value) here are referred for any Wald Tests conducted on the coefficients to see if each covariate has influence on the expected survival time.")


aft_model4 <- survreg(Surv(survival_time, survival_status) ~ Diseasegroup + CD34kgx10d6 + Diseasegroup * CD34kgx10d6, data = patients, dist = "lognormal")
summary(aft_model4)

lrt_sum <- anova(aft_model2, aft_model3, aft_model4)

lrt_sum <- lrt_sum[,c(-2,-4,-6)]

lrt_sum[,1] = c("Malignance","Malignance + CD34+","Malignance*CD34")
CompareTo<- c("NA", "Malignance", "Malignance+CD34+")
lrt_sum <- cbind(lrt_sum, CompareTo)
kable(lrt_sum, caption = "LRT Summaries of Parametric Models", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 12) %>% footnote(general = "This table shows the Likelihood Ratio Test (LRT) results including the test statistics $-2*LL$, the degrees of freedom $Df$ and the corresponding p-values $Pr(>Chi)$ under chi-square distributions for selecting the parametric model. In this table, the model 3 with the interaction term also includes the main effects from previous model. The result shows that the second model is preferred compared to the third and first model.")

```
